headlines.summary: Iran is reviewing the latest U.S. ceasefire proposal, raising hopes of a truce extension and tempering oil prices as traders weigh the risk of renewed Middle East disruptions. headlines.title: Iraq, UAE, Saudi Arabia All Set for Major Oil Output Surge in 2027

headlines.title: IEA: Global Oil Stocks on Track for Historical Lows Ahead of Summer Peak
headlines.summary: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has warned that global oil markets could enter a "red zone" in July and August as rapidly depleting crude inventories coincide with the onset of peak summer fuel demand. headlines.title: HSBC Flags a Super-Squeeze In the Oil Market
headlines.summary: The Middle East crisis and the still closed Strait of Hormuz have created a “super-squeeze” in oil markets, which could lead to sharp price spikes, analysts at HSBC say. headlines.title: China Solar Makers Launch Space Energy Development Alliance
headlines.summary: China’s solar manufacturing industry on Tuesday launched a so-called Space Energy Development Alliance as the Chinese solar sector looks to conquering new frontiers amid oversupply at home. headlines.title: Venezuela Oil Exports Hit 7-Year High
headlines.summary: Venezuela’s oil exports inched up from April to hit a fresh seven-year high in May as shipments to the United States and India continued to rise. headlines.title: $90 Oil Could Lift India’s Inflation to 4.8% and Slow GDP Growth

headlines.title: China’s LNG Imports Recover Ahead of Peak Cooling Season
headlines.summary: China imported 4.9 million tons of liquefied natural gas last month, a slight increase on an annual basis, Bloomberg reported today, citing shipping data.
